programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Lucy Adams is one of more than a million people in the UK with long Covid. She has been suffering a range of symptoms for more than a year and wants to know why. Panorama follows her as she speaks to doctors with increasing caseloads, leading scientists carrying out research and other patients desperate to know when they will be well again.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Mottingham in London, a detached house in Littleover in Derby, and a terrace in Dover in Kent are all renovated to a very high standard. The difference is the time scales to complete the work which run from under a week to more than two and a half years. Martin, Dion and Martel keep an eye on progress.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Leading barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. In the mid-19th century, a poison panic gripped Victorian Britain when a slew of murders, many perpetrated by women, caused national hysteria. Their weapon of choice was an easily available powdered poison called arsenic.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland twenty-three years ago is in A&E with a very sore shoulder. He fell against a fence and took out two fence-posts. Jean, the community nurse on the little archipelago of Out Skerries, is out on her rounds seeing patients and delivering medicine as a one-woman health service.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by the red and blue teams. It can be a jungle out there, so Eric offers advice on how to spot a bargain. He also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: This Is BBC One HD
programme.desc: Please switch over to BBC One.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is put in mortal danger when an attempt to lure the killer takes a deadly twist. Meanwhile, Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the killer's past, but can he get to Gina in time?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their most exotic Garden Rescue transformations, projects influenced by far-flung countries and cultures, from Malaysia to Zambia. They include a Caribbean garden full of tropical planting, an Eastern paradise with a centre-piece moon gate, a Japanese garden complete with a yoga deck, and a tiny backyard transformed in the style of the Mughal Empire.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who want to turn the spectacular Lake District from a holiday destination into their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team are in the stunning Somerset levels, working with Vicki and Bryan. The couple dreams of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Captain Izzie Balmer and First Mate Charlie Ross are sailing to the Isle of Wight for their final leg of this Road Trip. Charlie rips his trousers while on horseback, and Izzie ditches the classic car in favour of a speedboat.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Death in Paradise

When Emily mysteriously disappears one day, Stephanie launches her own investigation by digging into her friend's past, finding a few surprises along the way.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Christa Larwood is in Northern Ireland for the final part of the Road to Recovery series. Driving up the coast in a modern electric version of a classic British vehicle, she hears stories of resilience and recovery as tourism begins to emerge from the pandemic. Along the way she samples a local delicacy from a family forced to adapt their oyster farm, searches for puffins on an island relatively isolated during the crisis and hears how even Giant's Causeway was affected by the pandemic.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha are joined by special guest Miles Jupp. There are great moments from the BBC food archive, including clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nigella Lawson and Mary Berry, and drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Soothing and much-loved home comfort delights, from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart including eggs benedict florentine, fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of cave-man cooking with her, and enjoys his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouth-watering Swedish donuts.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Wales are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners and hear their plans which range from creating a family home for themselves, to extending a school and renovating for the rental market.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: BBC Weekend News

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha and the team are in Ardingly at the South of England Showground. The reds and blues battle it out around the fair with Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley. Meanwhile, Natasha shows off a rather quirky find with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: Live: Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces live coverage as St Helens bid to make it lucky number 13 in the Challenge Cup final against Castleford Tigers. Twelve-time winners Saints have a rich Challenge Cup pedigree, but the reigning Super League champions have not won the showpiece final since 2008. Victory here would cap a 2021 double for Saints after their women's team beat York City Knights earlier in the year at the Leigh Sports Village.

programme.title: Young Guns

programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate. With Steve Rotherham.

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: A thought-provoking debate and instant audience reaction on the big ethical talking points of the week, together with roving reports from around the UK.

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Onaivi family are well and truly at odds when to comes to where they want to spend their lives. Mum Krystyn wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. But husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perthgives them the chance to look at the homes, jobs and the lifestyle on offer.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team are on the Welsh island of Anglesey. The reds and blues go head-to-head at the antiques fair with experts Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley. The teams have only one hour to buy three items to sell for profit at auction.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in Arundel in West Sussex, home to the Dukes and Duchesses of Norfolk. She visits the Roman Catholic cathedral built by the fifteenth duke as a statement of his faith, and she explores the stunning castle gardens. Radzi Chinyanganya is invited to the Convent of the Poor Clares in Arundel to hear how they came to top the charts with their classical album, and we find out about Wheels for the World, a Christian project transforming lives by restoring wheelchairs.

programme.title: Live: International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha introduces live coverage from Headingley as England and Pakistan meet in the second of a three-match Twenty20 series. With August's Test series against India looming large, the England squad will be anxious for a strong showing in Leeds.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a £6,000 Italian garden for a couple in Fareham. The designers' creative skills are going to be tested to the limit if the uninspiring grass patch is to become a Mediterranean idyll.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap head to Yorkshire's Flamborough Head. Anita visits England's largest onshore seabird colony at Bempton Cliffs, helping track breeding numbers and witnessing a very special arrival as a rare Albatross pays a visit. Below the cliffs, Tom explores some of the coast's chalk caves, discovering the rare life they support, and Tom and Anita are joined by local youngsters as they go on a rockpool safari to take stock of the marine life washed up at low tide.
programme.category: Natuur en milieu, Landbouw, Dieren, Reizen, Outdoor, Nieuws

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team make a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the West Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in WWII and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona Bruce looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait.

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: Julien Baptiste travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador, Emma Chambers, find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressure is on to save Emma's two sons.

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem
programme.desc: Victorian London is gripped with fear as a serial killer is on the loose, leaving cryptic messages written in the blood of his victims. With few leads and increasing public pressure, Scotland Yard assigns the case to Inspector Kildare, a seasoned detective who has a sneaking suspicion that he's being set up to fail. Faced with a long list of suspects, Kildare must rely on help from a witness to stop the murders and bring the maniac to justice.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Olivia and Matt, a married couple from Worcester, face questions and big decisions as they bid to win money to help get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. But in a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen.

programme.desc: As a flood of women come forward to make anonymous complaints of sexual violence on campus, is this Scotland's #MeToo moment? Reporter Hazel Martin investigates what lies behind the complaints and asks whether enough is being done by universities to protect and support students under their care. The programme hears from young women who felt discouraged from reporting their assaults and looks at how seriously students are taken when they do complain.

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Leading criminal barristers Sasha Wass QC and Jeremy Dein QC revisit their investigation of an infamous Victorian poisoning case, where a wife was convicted of the murder of her husband. Liverpool, 1889. On 11 May, cotton broker James Maybrick passed away after a two-week battle with illness. A post-mortem revealed his body contained traces of the deadly poison arsenic. Suspicion soon fell on his wife, Florence Maybrick, after servants saw her handing some poisons.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E feeling very unwell. The team needs to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell go antique-hunting in Brackley, Northamptonshire, before heading to Stratford-upon-Avon for today's auction. Charlie seeks illumination about the life of Victorian designer Charles Kempe, famous for his stained glass.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, take a look at some of their favourite transformational Garden Rescues, the really tough jobs that had the biggest transformations by the team. There's a concrete back yard turned into a garden paradise with its own poppy meadow, a new build back garden that is turned into the ultimate outdoor party area and bar, and a soil-filled garden on a 45-degree slope that gets three tiers and a contemporary take on sheets of steel.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In England's smallest county, Rutland, a couple from Buckinghamshire with a budget of £500,000 ask Steve Brown and the team to find them a detached property with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by one of the country's best loved gardeners, the late Geoff Hamilton.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team are in the dramatic Black Mountains of Wales with Alex and Laura. The couple have a busy life with a craft cider business and a young family, and now they want to expand their enterprise to include unique accommodation.

programme.desc: All the latest on the Coronavirus pandemic as restrictions are eased in England, including a live news conference led by the Prime Minister.

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas are joined by Rufus Sewell, Lily Allen and former EastEnder Jake Wood, and Radio 1's Greg James, Jordan North and Vick Hope reveal where their show is going on its UK Summer Break Tour.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Tony and Nayna love being outdoors, but their new build garden in Liverpool is uninspiring. It is just grass with some planting, and the couple have run out of ideas. They are hoping for a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, and Tony dreams of a centrepiece water feature. It must have a multi-functional pergola for entertaining and for Tony to use his punchbag. Also, they want plenty of tropical planting. With a budget of £4,500, can Charlie and Lee punch above their weight?
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum becomes desperate when Ben refuses to listen to his pleas. Tiffany pushes Bernie to get an answer, and Lola comes to terms with her new reality.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Horse racing is one of the most popular and profitable sports, a £5 billion industry in the UK and Ireland followed by millions. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. Panorama discovers that off the track, many horses suffer career-ending injuries.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: As the London 2012 organisers face cynics foretelling international embarrassment, and the strategists at UK Sports try to work out how Team GB could beat the Olympic superpowers, the athletes prepare themselves for a competition that could define their legacy. Having unexpectedly come fourth in Beijing, to do even better at London meant defeating one of the three 'untouchable' sporting superpowers: the USA, China and Russia. The athletes would have to put in the performances of their lives.

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: After winning gold in Rio 2016, double Olympic champion Helen Glover decided to hang up the oars and step away from rowing to start a family. Helen had three children together with husband Steve Backshall, but the Covid-19 pandemic and ensuing lockdown brought on a change in perspective. Despite not having stepped foot in a boat since Rio, Helen decided to take a shot at a third Olympic gold.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris head to Cardiff to meet Steph and Matt, parents to seven-year-old William and four-year-old Alex, with another baby on the way. Matt and Steph are stuck in a food rut due to William's serious food allergies. Following an anaphylactic reaction at the age of three, William cannot eat certain foods, including nuts, sesame seeds and flax seeds. As a result, the family are brand-loyal to particular products that they know William can safely eat.

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Leading criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit the troubling case of a care home nurse convicted of poisoning a patient, a crime for which she would hang. Nottingham, 1935. Ada Baguley, a 55-year-old resident of a family-run nursing home, passed away. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Paramedics and the coastguard rescue team help a six foot nine inch Norwegian nicknamed Tiny off his tall ship. The sailor was on his way to the Shetland Folk Festival, but having a bad ankle fracture means he'll spend the night in hospital instead.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The team are at The Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams are joined by experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks. The reds and blues race around the fair hoping to bag themselves three items that will hopefully make them a profit at auction. Meanwhile Natasha pays a visit to The Jupiter Artland sculpture park in Edinburgh.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: In the early hours of 18 January 1981, in a house in south London, a birthday party ended in a fire. Thirteen young black British people died. The fire and its aftermath would ignite an uprising by the black British community. This film tells the stories of the young people who were at the party and the events that led up to it.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous

The trio love living in Cardiff for its great nightlife and the hustle and bustle of living in the capital. They are big personalities, but their living space does not represent them.

In the early hours of 6 July, Martha Brown, a resident of Birdsmoorgate in rural Dorset, called on her neighbour begging for help.

There's also plenty of brilliant new gardening tips from Charlie and the boys.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: First through the doors is Annalisa, with a unique piece of farming history. Also facing the dealers are mother and daughters Jennifer, Ann and Gina, with a fascinating tribal mask bought from their local school.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First, art conservator Lucia Scalisi faces the thrilling task of restoring a rare 17th-century painting of Queen Henrietta Maria, the wife of Charles I. For owner Howard Bird, the painting is invaluable as it represents a lifeline between himself and a dear friend who sadly passed away. As Lucia scales away the layers of dark overpaint, she makes a discovery.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: The story of the aftermath of the New Cross fire and the run up to the Black People's Day of Action. As news spread about the fire at 439 New Cross Road, the scale of the tragedy overwhelmed the local community. Amid uncertainty about whether the fire had been caused by a racist firebomb attack, anger mounted at the police investigation and the seeming indifference of the press and the government to the loss of so many black lives.

programme.title: Jade Jones: Fighting for Gold
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of double Olympic taekwondo gold medalist Jade Jones as she prepares to try to make history by becoming the first British woman to win gold medals in three successive games. The film offers a highly personal insight into the life of the tough, competitive and uncompromising girl from Flint. Jade, her friends, her family and her trainers reveal how a troubled youth dominated by shoplifting and problems at school was transformed through her love of sport.

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy's Secret World of Japanese Bicycle Racing
programme.desc: Olympic icon Sir Chris Hoy heads to Japan, host of the 2021 Tokyo Games, to explore the phenomenon of the keirin, the most extreme and exciting event in track cycling. A test of controlled pacing then a tactical fight to the line, keirin is a sport in its own right in its country of origin and is steeped in the history and rituals that make Japanese culture so unique.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: As the London 2012 organisers face cynics foretelling international embarrassment, and the strategists at UK Sports try to work out how Team GB could beat the Olympic superpowers, the athletes prepare themselves for a competition that could define their legacy. Having unexpectedly come fourth in Beijing, to do even better at London meant defeating one of the three 'untouchable' sporting superpowers: the USA, China and Russia. The athletes would have to put in the performances of their lives.

Penge, 1877. On 12 April a desperately frail woman was taken on an arduous journey from rural Kent to Penge on the outskirts of south east London. That woman was 36-year-old wealthy heiress Harriett Staunton. Moaning and barely conscious, she had to be carried the final stretch to her lodging house.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: After the New Cross fire and the Black People's Day of Action, tensions between the community and the police escalated when a massive stop-and-search operation was launched, targeting black people on the streets of Brixton. In April, the situation boiled over into one of the biggest riots in British history. Buildings were burned down and hundreds of police injured. Riots then flared up all over the country, from Southall to Toxteth.

programme.title: Live: Olympic Games
programme.desc: Team GB's men's road cyclists go for gold, while Max Whitlock is among the British gymnasts competing for places in both team and individual finals in Tokyo. Britain's four-man road race team in Tokyo includes three Grand Tour champions, including Tour de France winner Geraint Thomas, Vuelta winner Simon Yates and Giro d'Italia winner Tao Geoghegan Hart. Whitlock famously won double Olympic gold in Rio and will be aiming for more medals in Tokyo.

programme.title: Live: Olympic Games
programme.desc: Adam Peaty begins his campaign to become the first Briton to retain an Olympic swimming title, while the men's road race champion will be crowned in the shadow of Mount Fuji. Peaty has dominated the men's 100m breaststroke since winning the Olympic title in Rio, and became the first man to break the 57-second barrier at the 2019 World Championships. Earlier this year, he retained his European title, and he will be looking to lay down another marker in today's heats.

programme.title: Live: Olympic Games
